title: I’ve been building an AI trading dashboard/bot for 2 years — looking for honest feedback
author: johnmagus
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I’ve been building a trading technology project called Magus Prime X.

It started as a simple signal bot, but it has now evolved into a full AI-assisted trading dashboard designed to help traders analyze markets, track signals, manage risk, and monitor trades from one place.

Magus Prime X is built around one main idea:

Trading automation should not be a blind black box.

A trader should be able to see what the system detected, why a signal was approved, which strategy was involved, what risk settings were applied, and how the trade is being monitored after execution.

MPX combines multiple layers into one dashboard:

AI-assisted market analysis

Multi-strategy signal engine

Smart signal approval system

Guardian risk monitoring

Trade lifecycle tracking

Strategy performance tracking

Telegram signal delivery

Broker integration structure

Live chart workspace

Per-user trading settings

Manual / AI-assist / Auto mode structure

SBB learning system for reviewing signal outcomes

The system is designed to support different types of strategies and market logic, including:

Trend continuation

Scalping

Liquidity grabs

Support and resistance reactions

Smart Money Concepts

Fair Value Gaps

Order blocks

ORB

Volatility-based logic

AI/news context

The focus is not on hype, fake profit screenshots, or “guaranteed wins.”

The focus is on building a serious trading assistant that gives traders more structure, more visibility, and better control over their trading process.

What MPX helps with:

Organizing signals clearly

Showing why a trade setup exists

Tracking the full trade lifecycle

Monitoring risk conditions

Separating manual, AI-assisted, and automated trading modes

Keeping strategy performance visible

Making the trading dashboard feel cleaner and more professional

I’m currently opening free early access for people who want to try the platform, explore the dashboard, and give practical feedback from a trader or builder point of view.

There is no need to subscribe right now.

This is mainly for early testers who want to see the system, test the flow, and help shape the next version before the wider release.

If you are interested in AI trading tools, trading dashboards, signal systems, or algo-trading products, feel free to comment or message me and I’ll share access.

Would love to hear thoughts from traders, developers, and people building in this space.
